chunks.text: Pieter Abbeel is a Professor at UC Berkeley, Director of the Berkeley Robot Learning Lab and Co-Director of the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence (BAIR) Lab. His contact information includes office at 746 Sutardja Dai Hall, phone (510) 642-7034, fax (510) 643-1534, and email pabbeel@cs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are arranged via email, and his research assistant is Blake Greenwood (bgreenwood@berkeley.edu).

Professor Pieter Abbeel's research focuses on building intelligent systems through deep reinforcement learning, deep imitation learning, deep unsupervised learning, transfer learning, meta-learning, and learning to learn. He also studies AI's societal impact and its applications in other disciplines. His educational materials are widely used, and he has founded companies like Gradescope, Covariant, and Berkeley Open Arms. Abbeel advises AI startups and is a frequent speaker on AI strategy. He has received numerous awards, including PECASE, NSF-CAREER, and TR35, and his work is featured in major media outlets.

Pieter Abbeel earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2008 and an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from KU Leuven, Belgium in 2000. His research areas include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), and Machine Learning. He is associated with research centers such as the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD), Center for Automation and Learning for Medical Robotics (Cal-MR), Center for Human Compatible Artificial Intelligence (CHAI), and CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR).

Selected publications by Pieter Abbeel and collaborators include works on robot learning from human experience, reinforcement learning for robotic assembly, surgical trajectory segmentation, haptic probes for surgery, grasp planning under uncertainty, cloud robotics, path planning with uncertainty, autonomous helicopter aerobatics, cloth grasp detection, and GPS software receivers. These publications span from 2009 to 2025, covering various conferences and journals in robotics and AI.

Pieter Abbeel has received numerous honors and awards, including the IEEE Kiyo Tomiyasu Award (2022), ACM Prize in Computing (2021), IEEE Fellow (2018), Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching (2018), Google Faculty Research Award (2018), CRA-E Undergraduate Research Faculty Mentoring Award (2016), Bakar Fellows Spark Award (2015), DOD Presidential Early Career Award (2013), DARPA Young Faculty Award (2013), RAS Early Career Award (2012), Hellman Fellow (2011), Sloan Research Fellow (2011), MIT Tech Review TR35 (2011), and Okawa Research Grant (2010).
